project.name: AestheticMed Global Corporate Site
project.description: Rediseño completo de www.termosalud.com como plataforma corporativa global multi-mercado y multi-idioma, con API REST desde el inicio para soportar web, apps móviles y futuras integraciones. Incluye contenidos regulados (intended use, MDR, FDA), catálogo de equipos médicos y accesorios, motor unificado de contenidos (blog, noticias, prensa), layouts personalizados, landings con URLs libres, y gestión dinámica de mercados e idiomas. Arquitectura hexagonal con core desacoplado en src/ para independencia del framework.

business: Position the brand as a global leader in medical aesthetics in each key market.
Generate high-quality leads for clinics, distributors and investors worldwide.
Support sales, training and regulatory compliance with robust documentation.
technical: Core business logic in src/ with hexagonal architecture, fully decoupled from Laravel framework.
REST API from day one to support web, mobile apps (Ionic/React Native), and future integrations.
Laravel 11 as infrastructure layer only - routes, controllers, Eloquent repositories.
Vue 3 + TypeScript + Inertia + TailwindCSS for web frontend.
Unified content engine (ContentArticle) for blog, news, and press - same model, different categories.
Explicit routing without accidental complexity - no generic slug resolution with multiple database checks.
Dynamic management of markets and languages from backoffice/API without code changes.
Custom landing pages with free URLs and block-based layouts.
Corporate content sharing across markets to avoid duplication.
Achieve top Core Web Vitals on all core templates.
seo: Maximize organic visibility per country and per language (multi-regional + multilingual).
Expose markets and languages in the URL following international SEO best practices (Siemens-style).
Ensure 100% friendly and 100% localizable URLs: every visible segment is translatable.
Implement hreflang per language-region combination (es-ES, es-MX, en-US, fr-FR, etc.).

architecture.philosophy: Hexagonal Architecture with framework-agnostic core. Business logic in src/ with modules at root level (no BoundedContexts folder). Shared/ contains reusable domain primitives and infrastructure. Inspired by real production architecture from TermoExpertis.

structure.src/: Domain modules at root level (Catalog/, Content/, etc.) + Shared/
structure.src/Shared/: Reusable domain primitives (AggregateRoot, ValueObjects, Bus, Criteria)
structure.app/: Laravel infrastructure layer - HTTP, Eloquent, Jobs, Events, etc.

backend.role: Infrastructure adapter ONLY - HTTP routing, Eloquent ORM, Queue, Cache, Events
modules: Catalog (Products - Domain Entity with regulatory variants)
Content (ContentArticle unified for blog/news/press + CorporatePage + CustomLanding)
Forms (TermoCRM-based pattern: Form + FormSubmission + FormSubmissionResponse, Anti-spam 6-layer, MiTermosalud CRM OAuth2 integration)
Auth (Users, roles, OAuth2 Passport for backoffice)

layers.domain: src/{Module}/Domain - Aggregates, Value Objects, Domain Events, Repository Interfaces (Ports)
layers.application: src/{Module}/Application - Use Cases organized by action (Create/, Find/, Update/, Delete/)
layers.infrastructure: src/{Module}/Infrastructure - Repository implementations (Eloquent Adapters), External Services
layers.shared: src/Shared/ - Reusable base classes (AggregateRoot, ValueObjects, Bus, Criteria, Helpers)

notes: Modules at root level of src/, no nested BoundedContexts/ folder for cleaner paths.
Shared/ folder is critical: contains AggregateRoot, ValueObjects base classes, Bus (CQRS), Criteria patterns.
Backoffice is NOT a module - it's an infrastructure orchestration layer (API + Web UI).
Content module handles blog, news, press with unified ContentArticle entity (type field).
Each module is 100% framework-independent. Laravel is just infrastructure.
Repository interfaces (Ports) in Domain, Eloquent implementations (Adapters) in Infrastructure.
Application layer organizes use cases by action: Create/, Find/, Update/, Delete/ folders.
Dependencies flow inward: Infrastructure -> Application -> Domain -> Shared. Domain has no external deps.
Architecture inspired by real production system (TermoExpertis) - pragmatic DDD, not academic.

api.authentication: Laravel Passport OAuth2 (Client Credentials for backoffice, public endpoints for forms)
consumers: Future mobile apps (Ionic, React Native) - OAuth2

benefits: SEO: Google ve HTML completo pre-renderizado

whyInertiaNotNuxt: Laravel ya es backend sólido (hexagonal architecture en src/)
No duplicar lógica: auth, routing, middleware ya en Laravel
SSR simple sin Node server en producción (opcional)
40% menos código (no API REST para todo)
Pre-fetch automático en hover (Core Web Vitals)
whenNuxtWouldBeNeeded: Frontend completamente desacoplado (hoy Laravel, mañana otro backend)
Multiple clients distintos (web + mobile app con mismo frontend)

mobile.planned: Ionic or React Native consuming REST API (/api/v1/*)
mobile.sharing: Same API endpoints as web, different UI

i18n.description: Languages are explicit in the URL, separate from market/country. All content types and path segments are fully localizable. Languages can be added, edited, activated/deactivated from backoffice without code changes.
principles: Language segment is always the second path segment: /{marketCode}/{language}/...
All translatable fields (titles, descriptions, section names, slugs) exist per language.
Fallbacks are allowed from language-region to base language, but URLs remain language+market specific for SEO.

marketsConfig.description: Definition of markets/countries and their relationship with languages and regulation regions. Markets are fully manageable from backoffice - can be added, edited, activated/deactivated dynamically without code changes.
detectionRules: Market is the first URL segment: /{marketCode}/... (e.g. /es/, /mx/, /us/).
If a user lands on the root domain, detect country by GeoIP and redirect (with 302) to the best market-language combo or show a country selector.
Internally, each market maps to a regulatoryRegion (FDA, EU_MDR, LATAM_GENERIC, etc.).

urlStrategy.description: Siemens-style, 100% friendly, 100% localizable URLs with explicit market and language segments.

segments.marketCode: Two-letter lowercase country/market code (es, mx, us, fr, de, ...).
segments.language: Two-letter lowercase language code (es, en, fr, de, it, pt, lt).
segments.contentSlugLocalized: Localized content slug for product, treatment, article, etc.

rules: Every public page URL includes both marketCode and language for maximum international SEO clarity.
Section segments are ALWAYS fully localized per language (no internal keys or English-only words forced).
Content slugs are localized per language.
When a section slug changes, a 301 redirect is created from old to new URL automatically.
When a content slug changes, a 301 redirect is created from old to new URL automatically.
No query parameters or hashes are required to differentiate language/market; everything is in the path.

examples.description: Product page – Spain, Spanish (Zionic Pro Max)
Product page – Mexico, Spanish (Zionic Pro Max)

sectionSlugConfig.description: Mapping between internal section keys and localized URL segments per language. Blog, news, and press share the same ContentArticle backend but have different URL sections.
rules: Localized section slugs are defined per language, not per market; same language can be reused across markets.
Backoffice can update localized section slugs; system generates 301 redirects from old to new URLs.
Routing uses the internalKey + language to resolve the correct localized section slug.

contentModel.description: Separation of global entities, regulatory variants, and localized content to avoid duplication while supporting per-market regulation. Unified content engine: ContentArticle handles blog, news, and press (differentiated by type/category). All content types managed through block-based CMS with per-page layouts. Corporate content shareable across markets. Custom landing pages with free URLs.

contentTypes.description: Blog, News, and Press use the same ContentArticle entity, differentiated by 'type' field.

types.label: Blog Post
types.description: Long-form educational content, thought leadership, case studies

types.label: News Article
types.description: Corporate news, product launches, company announcements

types.label: Press Release
types.description: Official press releases, media coverage, press clippings

contentTypes.rationale: Same data model (title, body, author, categories, tags, images) but different presentation and URL sections. Avoids code duplication and simplifies backoffice management.

formsPattern.description: Forms system based on proven TermoCRM production pattern (3-entity architecture)

entities.name: Form
entities.description: Form template with dynamic structure stored in elements JSON field
fields: elements - JSON array with form structure (field types, validation, labels)
features: Dynamic form structure (no migrations for new fields)
Validation rules defined in elements JSON

entities.name: FormSubmission
entities.description: Instance of a form submission from public website
fields: name - Submission name (from form or generated)
ipAddress, userAgent, pageUrl, referrer - Tracking fields
spamFlags - JSON array of spam indicators
status - ENUM (pending, validated, notified, sent_to_crm, failed, spam)
crmLeadId, crmSentAt, crmResponse - CRM integration fields
differences_from_termocrm: NO polymorphic relationship (public forms, not attached to entities)
ADDED tracking fields (IP, UA, URL, referrer)
ADDED status enum for workflow tracking

entities.name: FormSubmissionResponse
entities.description: Individual field response (one record per form field)
features: Flexible schema (no rigid columns per field)
Queryable by field values (indexed on name, value)
Stores field configuration with response for audit

antiSpamSystem.description: 6-layer anti-spam protection (not in TermoCRM, added for public web)
layers: 1. Honeypot field (hidden field that bots fill)
2. Time throttle (min 3 seconds between page load and submit)

crmIntegration.description: Background integration with MiTermosalud CRM via OAuth2
flow: 5. CRM responds with lead ID (status: sent_to_crm)
6. Retry 3 times on failure (status: failed after 3 attempts)

validationFlow.description: Dynamic validation from Form.elements JSON (TermoCRM pattern)
example: Form.elements contains: [{name: 'email', type: 'email', required: true, validation: 'email'}]
After validation passes, Creator service creates FormSubmission + individual FormSubmissionResponse per field

rules: Markets and Languages are fully configurable entities managed from backoffice.
Each Market can have multiple Languages enabled via MarketLanguage relation.
Each logical entity (Product, Treatment, etc.) has one ContentMaster per language.
ContentMarketOverride exists only when a specific market requires changes to the base language content (claims, disclaimers, images, extra sections...).
Rendering merges ContentMaster(language) + ContentMarketOverride(marketCode) when override exists.
URLs are always generated as /{marketCode}/{language}/{localizedSection}/{slugLocalized}.
If no override exists for a given market, ContentMaster(language) is used as-is (still under the market+language URL).
Every ContentMaster(language) can be enabled or disabled per market via ContentMarketLanguageAvailability.
If ContentMarketLanguageAvailability.isEnabled=false for a given market+language, the corresponding URL /{market}/{language}/... returns 404 or a controlled 'not available in this market' page.
isIndexed allows disabling indexation (noindex) for specific market+language combinations while keeping the content visible.

All content types (products, treatments, content articles, corporate pages, landings) follow the same scheme: global entity -> ContentMaster(language) -> MarketOverride (optional) -> MarketLanguageAvailability.
ContentArticle is a unified entity for blog, news, and press - same model, different 'type' field and URL patterns.
CorporatePage can be marked as isSharedAcrossMarkets=true, meaning the same content appears in all markets (or restricted list) without duplication - only one ContentMaster per language is needed.
CustomLanding entities have fully customizable URLs (/{marketCode}/{language}/{customSlug}) outside predefined sections, ideal for promotions, events, webinars.
CustomLanding can be market-specific or language-specific, allowing flexible campaign targeting.
ProductRegulatoryVariant incluye campos para intended use, indicaciones, contraindicaciones y referencias explícitas a MDR (Reglamento (UE) 2017/745) y FDA (21 CFR, tipo de vía regulatoria).
Los equipos (EQUIPMENT) y accesorios (ACCESSORY) se modelan como Product con productType distinto, pudiendo relacionarse mediante ProductAccessoryRelation.
La web soporta marketing de contenidos con taxonomías de ContentCategory y ContentTag, traducibles por idioma, reutilizables en blog, noticias, productos, tratamientos y landings.
Las categorías y tags se usan para navegación temática, recomendaciones internas y señal fuerte de SEO (URLs, breadcrumbs, meta, schema.org).

productTyping.description: Tipificación de productos para distinguir equipos principales, accesorios, consumibles y software.
rules: EQUIPMENT: equipos principales de tratamiento (ej. Zionic Pro Max, Zionic, Eneka Pro).
ACCESSORY: manípulos, aplicadores, piezas de mano, kits complementarios asociados a uno o varios equipos.
CONSUMABLE: geles conductores, electrodos, fundas, elementos de un solo uso o consumo recurrente.
SOFTWARE: módulos de software independientes o add-ons de los equipos.

examples.name: Zionic Pro Max
examples.description: Equipo de radiofrecuencia multipolar + ultrasonidos para tratamientos corporales y faciales

hreflangStrategy.description: Generate hreflang tags per market+language combination to maximize Google understanding and avoid duplication.
rules: For each canonical URL /{marketCode}/{language}/... generate a hreflang tag language-region derived from language + market.hreflangRegion.
Example: /es/es/... => hreflang='es-ES', /mx/es/... => hreflang='es-MX', /us/en/... => hreflang='en-US'.
All alternate versions of the same logical page (same product or article) must reference each other in hreflang tags.
Define an x-default URL for global fallback (e.g. /us/en/ or /es/es/) for generic queries.
Generate XML sitemaps per market+language combination.
Include only published URLs (approved by Quality workflow).
Update sitemaps when slugs or section names change.

productLayouts.description: mi.com-style product pages with per-market, per-language layout flexibility.

workflow.description: Quality-gated publishing workflow to ensure regulatory compliance.
keyRules: Marketing and Communication edit content in draft state.
Any content affecting regulated information must go through pending_quality_review.
Only Quality (and Admin exceptionally) can move content to approved or published.
Public site always reads from the latest published version only.
Version history is kept for all regulated content, with field-level diffs (claims, disclaimers, layouts...).

routingStrategy.description: Explicit routing without accidental complexity. Each section has dedicated route handler.
routingStrategy.rationale: No generic slug resolution with multiple DB checks. Each route knows exactly where to look. Fast, cacheable, debuggable.

notes: TailwindCSS is the primary styling system; Bootstrap is not used.
Custom CSS per page or layout SHOULD be implemented as small Tailwind layer extensions or component-level styles whenever possible.

layoutSystem.description: Global layout system to allow each page (product, treatment, blog, corporate, landing) to have its own design, blocks, custom CSS/JS, Tailwind-based, mi.com-style.
jsAndCssRules: Each Layout can define customCss and customJs, loaded only on pages using that layout.
customJs should be modular, scoped to data-layout-id attributes to avoid global pollution.
TailwindCSS is the base design system; customCss should extend components or utilities rather than redefining global styles.
Layout-level tailwindSafelistJson defines additional classes to be kept during purge to support highly dynamic blocks.
usageRules: Any page type (product, treatment, blog post, corporate page, generic landing) can reference a specific Layout.
If no specific Layout is assigned, a default template per content type and per market+language is used.
Blog posts can either use a common blog layout template or a fully custom Layout, allowing hero images, galleries, jumbotrons, etc.
Layouts themselves are subject to the same Quality workflow as content (draft, pending_quality_review, approved, published).
